rationale: The only substantive ground-truth concern is yenuo26's inline question about whether reduce_scatter also needs test verification alongside device_communicator; X independently flags that the same test omits equivalent device_communicator/reduce_scatter assertions for _TP, landing squarely in the same theme, while Y's review never touches test-coverage gaps for these new assertions at all. X also delivers four other grounded, file:line-anchored findings (stale comments, missing docstring, unguarded cpu_group precondition) that are all verifiable against the diff, giving it much higher actionability than Y's single vague 'worth a one-line comment' note. Y's review is accurate but shallow — mostly a validation/blocker-scan with almost no concrete, actionable line-level findings, so it scores lower on recall and actionability despite reasonable precision.
